title: AI Fundamentals, Machine Learning, and Learning from Data

learning_objectives: Understand how AI differs from traditional software.

blocks.title: Week 4 Focus
blocks.content: Students compare rules-based software with learning-based systems.

items.label: AI
items.description: Describe AI as a broad field.

items.label: Prediction
items.description: Understand that ML systems predict or classify based on examples.

items.label: Traditional Software
items.description: Uses direct rules created by a programmer.

items.label: Machine Learning
items.description: Learns from examples instead of only fixed rules.

items.label: Training Data
items.description: The examples the system uses to learn patterns.

items.label: Prediction
items.description: The output created after learning from data.

blocks.title: Responsible Framing
blocks.content: Students should leave knowing AI is neither magic nor automatically trustworthy.

activity.title: AI Concept Classifier
activity.instructions: Match each concept to its description.

questions.prompt: What makes machine learning different from fixed-rule software?
questions.explanation: Machine learning models use examples to detect patterns and make predictions.

questions.prompt: Which example best fits machine learning?
options: A spam filter that improves from examples
questions.explanation: Spam filtering often depends on learned patterns from many examples.

options: It gives the system examples to learn from
questions.explanation: Training data is the raw material the model learns from.

reflection.prompt: Explain in simple words the difference between traditional software and machine learning.
reflection.placeholder: Traditional software follows direct rules. Machine learning learns patterns from examples and then makes predictions.

teacher_notes: Contrast a calculator with a recommendation system to keep the explanation grounded.
Avoid mathematics here; use examples, stories, and comparisons.
